message:
refactor(workspace): split recorder/session retention DTOs from execution (ft-xcsm0 / ft-8nqx0 Phase 4)

Phase 4 of the staged ft-8nqx0 extraction. Separates retention POLICY
DTOs from the operational deletion / cleanup execution paths so audit
and retention rules can be reviewed independently of live storage
mutation. Continues the Phase 1 / 3 / 5 work on the
`frankenterm-core-audit-types` leaf crate (ft-rqu5e / ft-mq7fl /
ft-nsoxc).

New modules in `frankenterm-core-audit-types/src/`:

- `recorder_retention_types.rs` (~453 LOC) — `RetentionConfig`,
  `RetentionError`, `SensitivityTier`, `SegmentPhase`, `SegmentMeta`,
  `RetentionStats`, `RetentionAuditEvent`, `RetentionAuditType`. Pure
  DTOs lifted from `frankenterm-core::recorder_retention`. Their
  validate / classify / lifecycle-transition impls move with them.
- `session_retention_types.rs` — `CleanupResult` (pure summary DTO)
  lifted from `frankenterm-core::session_retention`. The
  `cleanup_sessions` SQL pipeline stays in core.

What stays in `frankenterm-core`:

- `recorder_retention::RetentionManager` + `RetentionSweepResult`
  (operational engine: segment-list state, sweep orchestration). The
  manager now imports its DTOs via the leaf-crate re-export so its
  ~200 LOC of orchestration is the only thing left in the file
  alongside its tests.
- `session_retention::cleanup_sessions` and its SQL helpers
  (`delete_sessions_by_age`, `delete_excess_closed_sessions`,
  `delete_sessions_by_size`, `cleanup_orphaned_data`) — they own
  `rusqlite::Connection` + active-session safety checks per the
  proposal's "stays near storage" guidance.

Cross-crate dep added (`audit-types/Cargo.toml`):

- `frankenterm-core-replay-types` — `SensitivityTier::classify`
  consumes `RecorderRedactionLevel` which already lives in the
  `recorder_metadata` submodule of replay-types. Both crates are
  leaf-tier so this is leaf→leaf, no cycles.

Re-export contract preserved (`recorder_retention.rs` /
`session_retention.rs`):

- `pub use frankenterm_core_audit_types::recorder_retention_types::{
  RetentionAuditEvent, RetentionAuditType, RetentionConfig,
  RetentionError, RetentionStats, SegmentMeta, SegmentPhase,
  SensitivityTier };` — keeps `crate::recorder_retention::*` and
  `frankenterm_core::recorder_retention::*` paths resolving for the
  ~10 in-tree consumers (recorder_query, policy, recorder_migration,
  proptests).
- `pub use frankenterm_core_audit_types::session_retention_types::CleanupResult;`
  — same shape for the session_retention proptest + callers.

Verification:

- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core-audit-types` → clean (5.82s).
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` → clean
  (51.80s).
- `cargo check --workspace` (default features) → clean (1m29s).
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_session_retention
  --no-default-features` → clean (proptest still resolves
  `CleanupResult` via the re-export).

This closes the user-facing scope of the ft-8nqx0 staged extraction
(Phases 1/3/4/5 shipped). Phase 2 audit engines (ft-kldww) remain
open with the `policy_decision_log_engine` and `recorder_audit_engine`
pre-seeds already in this crate. Phase 5 storage audit boundary
(ft-4ses2) was started by linter co-extraction in the prior commit
and is partially landed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -18,3 +18,8 @@ workspace = true
 serde = { workspace = true, features = ["derive", "std", "alloc"] }
 serde_json.workspace = true
 tracing.workspace = true
+# ft-xcsm0 Phase 4: recorder_retention_types::SensitivityTier::classify
+# consumes RecorderRedactionLevel, which already lives in this leaf
+# crate alongside the rest of recorder metadata (replay-types is itself
+# leaf-tier so the dep is leaf→leaf, no cycles).
+frankenterm-core-replay-types = { path = "../frankenterm-core-replay-types" }

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+//! Session retention policy DTOs (ft-xcsm0 / ft-8nqx0 Phase 4).
+//!
+//! Lifted out of `frankenterm-core::session_retention` so the cleanup
+//! summary can be reviewed independently from the SQLite deletion
+//! pipeline (`cleanup_sessions`, `delete_sessions_by_age`,
+//! `cleanup_orphaned_data`, …) which still lives in core. The result
+//! shape is pure data — no `rusqlite::Connection`, no
+//! `crate::config`, just counters.
+
+/// Result of a session cleanup operation.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub struct CleanupResult {
+    /// Sessions deleted by age policy.
+    pub deleted_by_age: usize,
+    /// Sessions deleted by count limit.
+    pub deleted_by_count: usize,
+    /// Sessions deleted by size budget.
+    pub deleted_by_size: usize,
+    /// Orphaned checkpoint rows cleaned.
+    pub orphaned_checkpoints: usize,
+    /// Orphaned pane_state rows cleaned.
+    pub orphaned_pane_states: usize,
+    /// Whether VACUUM was run.
+    pub vacuumed: bool,
+}
+
+impl CleanupResult {
+    /// Total number of sessions deleted.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn total_sessions_deleted(&self) -> usize {
+        self.deleted_by_age + self.deleted_by_count + self.deleted_by_size
+    }
+
+    /// Whether any cleanup was performed.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn any_work_done(&self) -> bool {
+        self.total_sessions_deleted() > 0
+            || self.orphaned_checkpoints > 0
+            || self.orphaned_pane_states > 0
+    }
+}
